data.title: Why Bitcoin Is Up Today? Trump’s UK Trade Deal Tease Sparks Rally
data.description:
The sudden rally appears to tie directly to U. S. President Donald Trump’s cryptic announcement of a “major trade deal” with a “highly respected country,” which The New York Times later identified as the United Kingdom
source: https://coinpedia.org/news/why-bitcoin-is-up-today-trumps-uk-trade-deal-tease-sparks-rally/

data.title: Trump Criticizes Federal Reserve Chair Powell Amid Economic Developments
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump recently criticized Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, referring to him as a 'fool' who lacks understanding. Despite this criticism, Trump expressed a personal liking for Powell.

Trump highlighted the significant drop in oil and energy prices, noting that costs across various sectors, including groceries and eggs, are decreasing. He emphasized the low inflation rates and the steady influx of tariff revenue into the United States, countering claims that economic measures were implemented too late. Trump encouraged the public to enjoy these economic conditions.

data.title: Indian listed company Jetking plans to raise funds to increase its BTC holdings, aiming to hold 18,000 BTC by 2030
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Harsh Bharwani, CEO of Indian listed company Jetking, said that the company plans to gradually increase its holdings of BTC through a variety of financing tools, with the goal of holding 18,000 BTC by 2030. He revealed that the fundraising will be expanded to about 180 BTC in the next six months, and the goal will be 1,800 BTC in the next year, and the final holding plan will be completed by 2030.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Markets React Positively to Trade Deal Speculation
data.description:
According to PANews, Singapore-based crypto investment firm QCP Capital reported that the U.S. Federal Reserve's FOMC meeting maintained interest rates as expected, emphasizing the resilience of the U.S. economy, a tight labor market, and inflation slightly above the 2% target. Despite trade tensions from U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff plans, Fed Chair Powell remained cautious, stating that patience is low-cost and did not specify the number of rate cuts this year, with guidance postponed to the June meeting. The market has already priced in expected rate cuts of 25 basis points in July, September, and December.

Early today, Trump hinted at a significant trade agreement, sparking risk appetite and speculation that the UK might be a potential trade partner. Although details were scarce, the prospect alone was enough to cause fluctuations in asset prices.

Cryptocurrencies responded swiftly and positively. Bitcoin rose by 2.74%, decisively reclaiming the psychological threshold of $99,000. Meanwhile, Ethereum surged 6.89% during the Asian trading session, breaking out of its three-week consolidation range between $1,700 and $1,900. In the options market, there was a notable increase in demand for call options, particularly those expiring in May and June. This flow of funds indicates growing optimism as traders position for further gains amid improving macroeconomic conditions.

Looking ahead to the U.S. market opening tonight, attention will be on whether this upward trend can continue or if a typical "buy the rumor, sell the news" pullback will occur once trade partners are officially confirmed. QCP remains tactically cautious, suggesting limited gains from chasing the current levels until Bitcoin's daily closing price stabilizes above $100,000.

data.title: Russia Expands Siberian Factories to Boost Explosive Production Amid Conflict
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent government documents and satellite images reveal that Russia is undertaking significant factory expansions in remote Siberia to increase the production of a powerful explosive used in artillery shells and other ammunition amid the ongoing conflict with Ukraine.

Moscow's possession of more artillery shells than Ukraine has been a crucial factor in its battlefield successes during the conflict, where both sides primarily rely on artillery and drones. Three defense analysts suggest that as Ukraine and its Western allies ramp up weapon production, Russia's project is expected to enhance explosive output, potentially strengthening Moscow's firepower significantly.

data.title: CatFee TRON Energy Leasing Platform has been newly upgraded, with over 10,000 service orders per day, saving over 300,000 TRX
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: CatFee, the TRON ecological energy leasing platform, recently completed a comprehensive system upgrade. The platform's average daily energy delivery service orders exceeded 30,000 orders, saving users more than 300,000 TRX gas fees per day, greatly improving users' transfer experience and cost control capabilities on the TRON network.
It is reported that CatFee is a C2C energy trading platform under 100cat. It is currently actively deploying the C-end energy wholesale market and matching energy leasing needs between users through matching transactions. With its stable supply mechanism and automated matching system, the platform has become one of the options for Web3 users to save gas fees when using TRC20 transfers and using TRON energy.

data.title: Michael Saylor Advocates Bitcoin Investment for Saudi Sovereign Wealth Fund
data.description: According to PANews, Michael Saylor, Chairman of the Board at Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy), suggested in an interview with Saudi National Television that the Saudi Sovereign Wealth Fund should invest in Bitcoin. Saylor emphasized that purchasing Bitcoin could potentially increase the fund's value significantly, stating, "If you want your money to grow a thousandfold, then buy Bitcoin." The Saudi Sovereign Wealth Fund is reported to have assets totaling $930 billion.
